In a Truth Social post, he stated that he answered all questions correctly on the Montreal Cognitive Assessment (MoCA), describing his performance as evidence of “extreme intelligence.” He also said this was his fourth time taking such a test and asserted that he had achieved either perfect scores or equivalent results on all previous attempts.
Trump used the post to criticize Democrats, referring to them with derogatory language and suggesting that cognitive testing should be mandatory for all presidential and vice-presidential candidates. His comments followed the release of a White House memo indicating that his cognitive function, as assessed by the MoCA, was within normal limits and that he received a perfect score on the exam.
Medical experts, however, have clarified that the MoCA i

item.title: Star Wars: The Mandalorian Faces Box Office Decline Amid Indie Horror Success
item.description: Disney's latest Star Wars theatrical release, 'The Mandalorian and Grogu,' experienced a dramatic 70% drop in its second weekend at the box office, placing it third behind two low-budget horror films directed by young YouTube creators. 'Backrooms,' directed by 20-year-old Kane Parsons, and 'Obsession,' directed by 26-year-old Curry Barker, have both captured significant attention from younger audiences under 35, particularly those under 25. Despite their modest production budgets of roughly $10 million and $1 million respectively, 'Backrooms' has already grossed $118 million globally, while 'Obsession' has earned $148 million worldwide. Analysts note that these indie horror successes are not cannibalizing each other’s audience but are instead benefiting from the viral influence of their creators’ online fanbases. Industry experts suggest that the Star Wars film may struggle to reach profitability solely through theatrical revenue, though merchandising and streaming platforms could miti

item.title: Study finds chimpanzees and bonobos form layered social networks similar to human friendship circles
item.description:
A new international study led by researchers from Utrecht University and Universidad Carlos III de Madrid suggests that chimpanzees and bonobos organize their social relationships in ways that closely resemble human friendship networks. By analyzing grooming behavior across 24 groups of great apes, scientists found that both species distribute their limited social time unevenly, forming layered social structures with a small number of close bonds and a larger set of weaker connections.
Grooming, a key social activity among apes, was used as the primary measure of social investment. Researchers applied mathematical modeling to understand how individuals allocate their time among group members. The results showed a consistent pattern: most apes focused their grooming efforts on a few preferred partners while still maintaining broader, less intense relationships within their group. This structure mirrors the way humans tend to maintain an inner circle of close friends while also sustaini

item.title: Trump praises Giants quarterback Jaxson Dart and comments on his potential
item.description: During a recent appearance on 'My View with Lara Trump,' former President Donald Trump spoke highly of New York Giants quarterback Jaxson Dart. Trump described Dart as handsome and conservative, noting his potential as a young player if he remains healthy. The comments came in the wake of criticism Dart received for introducing Trump at a public event in Suffern, New York. Some teammates, including Abdul Carter, expressed disagreement with Dart's decision, emphasizing that certain choices go beyond football and reflect personal values. Dart explained that his decision to introduce the president was influenced by his upbringing and family history of military and public service, highlighting his respect for the position regardless of political affiliation. Trump also touched on his previous electoral victory in 2024, claiming strong support across many U.S. counties. The discussion emphasized Dart's talent and potential impact on the Giants while acknowledging the political controversy s

item.title: First CRL trainset delivered in Singapore as MRT project advances toward 2030 launch
item.description:
The first trainset for Singapore’s upcoming Cross Island Line (CRL) has arrived in the country, marking a key milestone in the development of the nation’s newest MRT line. According to the Land Transport Authority (LTA), this is the first of 44 six-car trainsets that will eventually serve the fully underground line. The train was recently unloaded at Jurong Port, the standard facility used for receiving new MRT rolling stock in Singapore.
Earlier in April, Acting Minister for Transport Jeffrey Siow was given a preview of the CR151 trains during a visit to the Qingdao rail depot in China, where the trains are manufactured. The trains are being supplied under a $589 million contract awarded in June 2023 to a consortium that includes CRCC Qingdao Sifang.
The CR151 trains feature several modern design improvements aimed at enhancing passenger comfort and operational efficiency. Each car includes five doors on each side to facilitate faster boarding and alighting, similar to the trains us

item.title: Ken Paxton draws criticism after defending his record by comparing it to Trump in Fox News interview
item.description:
Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton faced widespread criticism and online mockery following a Sunday interview on Fox News' “Sunday Morning Futures” with host Maria Bartiromo. During the segment, Paxton discussed his Senate primary campaign against Democratic Texas lawmaker James Talarico, focusing on questions about his controversial political and personal history. When asked about his reputation and a Wall Street Journal description labeling him as “scandal-plagued,” Paxton deflected by claiming that similar accusations could be made about former President Donald Trump. His comment quickly drew attention on social media, where political analysts, commentators, and journalists interpreted his defense as an unusual or self-incriminating comparison.
Paxton’s political career has been marked by multiple controversies, including a high-profile impeachment proceeding in the Texas legislature related to allegations involving improper conduct and alleged favors for a real estate developer. He

item.title: Bill Cosby Denied New Trial After $19 Million Civil Sexual Assault Ruling
item.description: A Los Angeles County Superior Court judge has rejected Bill Cosby’s motion for a new trial in a civil sexual assault case, upholding a jury’s $19.25 million damages award to former waitress Donna Motsinger. Motsinger, now 84, accused Cosby of drugging and assaulting her in 1972 after he escorted her to one of his shows. Judge Bradley S. Phillips ruled that Cosby did not demonstrate any irregularities in the trial that would justify a new hearing, and that the damages were not excessive. Cosby, who has consistently denied all allegations, previously served three years in a Pennsylvania prison for unrelated sexual assault charges before that conviction was overturned in 2021. Cosby filed the motion for a new trial in early April 2026, but the court’s decision maintains the civil judgment. Attorneys for Cosby have not commented on the ruling. This decision underscores the legal challenges Cosby continues to face decades after the alleged incidents, highlighting the persistence of civil li

item.title: Paul Krugman warns about U.S. political instability and inequality in post-Trump era
item.description:
A Nobel Prize-winning economist, Paul Krugman, has issued a warning about the long-term political and economic consequences of the movement associated with former President Donald Trump. In comments shared via a Substack video, Krugman argued that the United States faces significant challenges in addressing the conditions that enabled Trump’s rise, particularly rising wealth inequality and concentrated political and economic power. He suggested that these structural issues must be confronted after Trump leaves office to prevent a recurrence of similar political movements.
Krugman criticized what he described as the enabling role of wealthy donors and institutional actors, including the Supreme Court, in supporting Trump’s political ascent. He argued that such actors were aware of the risks but proceeded nonetheless, contributing to what he characterized as a breakdown in institutional safeguards. The economist warned that the concentration of power in the executive branch, combined wi

item.title: Mike Pence criticizes Trump-backed victim compensation fund, prompting backlash from MAGA supporters
item.description:
Former U.S. Vice President Mike Pence has publicly criticized a proposed Trump administration initiative involving a nearly $1.8 billion compensation fund intended for individuals claiming harm from alleged government misconduct. Speaking on NBC’s “Meet the Press,” Pence described the plan as “deeply offensive” and urged the administration to abandon it entirely. The fund reportedly stems from a settlement linked to litigation involving the Internal Revenue Service and allegations surrounding the improper release of Donald Trump’s tax records.
Pence argued that the proposal represents a departure from traditional conservative principles and warned that it reflects a broader shift away from established norms within the political right. He characterized the initiative as an example of what he sees as problematic policy direction in the current political environment.
His remarks quickly triggered a strong reaction from supporters of the MAGA movement on social media platforms. Several p

item.title: Former U.S. Border Patrol Chief Faces Criticism Over Alleged Extremist Associations and Online Gesture
item.description:
A report published by Raw Story highlights controversy surrounding Gregory Bovino, the former U.S. Border Patrol chief under the Trump administration, after he was accused by a journalist of engaging in extremist symbolism and attending a far-right political gathering in Europe. According to journalist Charles Davis, Bovino shared a photo on social media appearing to show him making a hand gesture that critics interpreted as resembling a Nazi-style salute. Davis argued that the image, which he described as a “Hitlerian greeting,” was especially concerning given Bovino’s reported participation in a European conference linked to far-right ideology.
The event in question, held in Portugal, was described in the report as a “neo-fascist summit” focused on the concept of “remigration,” a controversial idea associated with the mass removal of immigrants and ethnic minorities. The article states that the gathering included several far-right activists, including individuals linked to conspirac

item.title: Possible Early Israeli Elections Unlikely to Alter Core Policies Toward Palestinians
item.description:
The article examines the political implications of the Israeli Knesset's vote to dissolve itself and potentially trigger early elections. The measure, introduced on May 20 and supported by 110 members without opposition, still requires additional readings before taking effect. If approved, elections could occur earlier than expected, potentially ending Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's current coalition government.
According to the article, the vote was influenced by disputes over military service exemptions for the ultra-Orthodox Haredi community and ongoing criticism of Netanyahu's leadership following the October 2023 attacks, hostage negotiations, and subsequent regional conflicts. Polling cited in the article suggests Netanyahu's Likud party would remain the largest political force but would likely need coalition partners to govern.
The central argument is that a change in government would not necessarily result in significant changes to Israeli policies toward Palestinians. T

item.title: BuzzFeed Challenges Readers to Identify Famous Movie Musicals in a Timed Trivia Quiz
item.description:
BuzzFeed published an entertainment-focused trivia quiz created by staff writer Sara Thompson that tests readers' knowledge of well-known movie musicals. The quiz features a selection of titles drawn from different eras and styles, including Broadway adaptations, classic musical films, and more recent cinematic hits. Participants are challenged to identify as many movie musicals as possible within a limited time frame of 75 seconds, adding a competitive and fast-paced element to the experience.
The article is designed primarily as interactive entertainment rather than traditional news reporting. Readers are invited to demonstrate their familiarity with popular musical films while racing against the clock. According to the description, the quiz includes recognizable productions that have left a significant mark on popular culture and musical cinema. The author also playfully notes that revisiting these titles is likely to leave participants thinking about or listening to the associated

item.title: Shooting near Watts vigil leaves one woman dead and another injured
item.description: Early Sunday morning, a shooting near a candlelight vigil in Watts resulted in one woman dead and another injured. According to the Los Angeles Police Department, officers responded to reports of gunfire just after 2 a.m. near the intersection of East 104th Street and Wilmington Avenue. A 30-year-old woman was found with a gunshot wound and was transported to a nearby hospital, where she later died. Around the same time, a 31-year-old woman arrived at a local hospital with a gunshot wound that police believe occurred in the same incident. The LAPD is actively investigating the shooting and searching for suspects, though no motive or suspect information has been released. The incident occurred on a residential block and reportedly disrupted a vigil taking place nearby. While violent crime in Los Angeles has decreased overall, including an 11% drop in homicides compared to the same period last year, shootings and fatalities still occur sporadically, particularly in the Southeast patrol a

item.title: Trump criticizes federal judge and plaintiff in dispute over proposed White House drone facility
item.description:
The article reports on a controversial social media post by Donald Trump in which he directly criticized and threatened a federal judge overseeing a legal challenge related to a proposed White House renovation project involving a drone facility. According to the report, Trump used Truth Social to target U.S. District Judge Richard Leon, a George W. Bush appointee, accusing him of mishandling the case and warning that he would be held responsible if any negative consequences arise from the litigation. Trump described the proposed project as a highly advanced “DronePort” intended to enhance national security and suggested that modern military threats require updated defenses for Washington, D.C.
In his post, Trump also criticized the female plaintiff who filed the lawsuit against the project, calling her a “serial plaintiff” and arguing that she lacks legal standing. He urged for the case to be dismissed immediately, framing the lawsuit as an obstruction to national security improvement

item.title: Sea cucumber tissue survives independently for years, revealing unusual regenerative behavior
item.description:
Researchers studying a species of sea cucumber, Psolus fabricii, have discovered an extraordinary case of long-lived biological tissue that continues to survive, grow, and self-repair years after being removed from the organism. According to a study published in the journal Science, a sample of amputated tissue has remained viable for at least three years while being maintained in natural seawater conditions. During this time, the tissue did not simply persist in a dormant state; instead, it showed signs of biological activity including growth, immune response, cellular reorganization, and nutrient absorption.
The findings surprised scientists because previous experiments on tissue survival typically rely on sterile, controlled laboratory environments. In contrast, this sample endured in an open seawater environment filled with microbes and other organisms, making its persistence even more remarkable. Researchers described the phenomenon as “naturally occurring tissue immortality,” th

item.title: Lindsey Graham Urges Trump to Separate Israel-Hezbollah Conflict From Ongoing Iran Negotiations
item.description:
Sen. Lindsey Graham publicly stated that he supports a potential agreement between the United States and Iran but argued that negotiations should not be tied to Israel’s military actions against Hezbollah in Lebanon. In a social media post, Graham said he had recently discussed the matter with President Donald Trump and backed efforts to secure Iranian commitments regarding its nuclear program, support for terrorism, and the opening of the Strait of Hormuz. However, he strongly opposed any agreement that would limit Israel’s ability to respond to attacks from Hezbollah or Hamas.
The debate centers on ongoing U.S.-Iran negotiations, in which Iran has reportedly demanded that Israel halt its military operations in Lebanon as a condition for progress. According to the article, Iran views an end to Israeli strikes in Lebanon as a non-negotiable requirement. Graham rejected that position, arguing that a ceasefire would allow Hezbollah to regroup and strengthen its military capabilities.
T

item.title: Explosives storage blast kills over 45 in northern Myanmar village near China border
item.description:
A powerful explosion on Sunday in Kaungtup village, located in Namhkam township in Myanmar’s Shan state near the Chinese border, killed more than 45 people and injured around 70 others, according to rescue workers and local reports. The blast occurred around midday in a building reportedly used to store explosives intended for mining and quarrying operations. Rescue teams and residents immediately began search operations amid widespread destruction.
By Sunday evening, one rescue worker reported that at least 46 bodies, including those of six children, had been recovered and sent for cremation, while 74 injured individuals were transported to a nearby township hospital. Another local rescuer estimated that the death toll could be closer to 40 with more than 100 homes damaged, reflecting inconsistencies in early reporting. Myanmar media outlets also cited figures ranging from 50 to 55 fatalities, while Chinese state media confirmed multiple casualties without providing exact numbers.
T

item.title: Economists warn AI-driven job losses could weaken consumer demand and destabilize the economy
item.description:
A warning from economist and political scientist Chris Blattman has drawn attention to a recent academic study examining the economic risks posed by rapid artificial intelligence adoption. The study, titled “The AI Layoff Trap” and authored by researchers Brett Hemenway Falk and Gerry Tsoukalas, argues that businesses may enter an “automation arms race” in which they increasingly replace human workers with AI systems to cut costs and remain competitive. While individually rational for firms, this behavior could collectively reduce consumer purchasing power, ultimately harming the broader economy.
The study’s model suggests that as companies automate more jobs, displaced workers lose income, leading to weaker demand for goods and services. This creates a feedback loop in which firms continue automating to stay competitive, even though the overall effect undermines market stability. According to the researchers, standard economic mechanisms such as wage adjustments, increased competitio

item.title: KC Green Settles Dispute with AI Startup Artisan Over 'This is Fine' Artwork
item.description: KC Green, the artist behind the iconic 'This is fine' meme, has resolved a dispute with AI startup Artisan over the use of his artwork. The conflict began when Artisan used a modified version of Green's character in advertisements for its AI assistant, Ava. In the ads displayed on buses and subways, the dog character appeared in flames, but the text was changed from 'This is fine' to 'My pipeline is on fire,' promoting Artisan's AI service. Green publicly criticized the company, stating his work was misappropriated similarly to how AI copies content, and encouraged his followers to deface the ads. He also expressed frustration at having to navigate legal channels rather than focusing on his comics. Artisan stated that it respected Green's work and subsequently reached an agreement. Under the settlement, Artisan removed the ads in New York and San Francisco, while Green took down his original social media post. Both parties expressed satisfaction with the resolution, which was reached q

item.title: YouTube creators achieve major box office success with two leading weekend films
item.description:
This TechCrunch article reports on an emerging trend in the film industry: successful feature films directed by creators who originally built their audiences on YouTube. Over a single weekend, the top two movies at the box office were both directed by former YouTubers, signaling a shift in how mainstream entertainment talent is being discovered and developed.
Leading the box office is “Backrooms,” directed by Kane Parsons, a filmmaker who originally gained attention through a YouTube series based on eerie found-footage storytelling inspired by internet lore. The film, produced by A24, is projected to earn approximately $81 million domestically in its opening weekend, setting a new record for the studio and surpassing the previous high set by “Civil War.” Its success demonstrates how online-native creators can translate viral concepts into major theatrical releases with wide commercial appeal.
In second place is “Obsession,” directed by Curry Barker, another YouTube-origin creator kno

item.title: Robert Reich on inequality, corporate power, and the economic conditions behind Trump-style politics
item.description:
In this opinion piece, Robert Reich argues that American politics cannot be understood separately from the structure of the U.S. economy. He points to recent economic data showing a widening gap between corporate profits and worker compensation. While wages and benefits grew modestly, corporate profits increased at a significantly faster rate, continuing a long-term trend that has accelerated since the 1980s and especially after the pandemic. According to Reich, this shift has resulted in workers receiving a shrinking share of national income, while wealth increasingly concentrates among capital owners.
He highlights that monopolization is a central driver of this imbalance, with large corporations across technology, finance, retail, energy, and healthcare exercising dominant control over markets, prices, and wages. These firms, he argues, also wield substantial political influence, making it difficult to separate public policy from private corporate interests. At the same time, union

item.title: Australian researchers train brain cells to play Doom
item.description:
Australian researchers have trained lab-grown human brain cells on a silicon chip to interact with the classic video game Doom, a milestone that highlights the growing field of biological computing. The cells, grown from stem cells and integrated into a device called CL1, were connected to a digital environment that translated the game into electrical signals the neurons could interpret. In response, the cells produced patterns of activity that controlled basic in-game actions such as moving and firing.
The work is notable not because the cells played the game well, but because they showed real-time adaptation and goal-directed learning. Researchers say this suggests living neurons can process feedback and adjust behavior in ways that may eventually be useful for more practical applications. Those possible uses include more advanced brain-inspired computing systems, robotics control, and other tasks where adaptive decision-making matters.
The project is still early-stage and experime

item.title: How a guitar maker’s supply chain investigation revealed connections to elephant-related environmental impacts
item.description:
In this opinion piece, Bob Taylor, co-founder of Taylor Guitars, reflects on his decades-long journey in building a global musical instrument company and the unexpected environmental implications hidden within its supply chain. Having started the company in 1974 at just 19 years old, Taylor describes how the business grew from a small, inexperienced operation into a large-scale manufacturer producing hundreds of guitars daily for customers worldwide. As the company matured, so did its responsibility to understand where its raw materials come from and what impact their sourcing has on the environment.
Taylor explains that a deeper investigation into the supply chain of key materials—particularly exotic tonewoods such as ebony—led him far beyond factories and distributors and into ecosystems where these resources originate. What began as a routine effort to ensure quality and legality in sourcing ultimately revealed a complex relationship between instrument manufacturing and wildlife ha

item.title: Roger Stone urges Trump to dismiss Smithsonian staff over disputed Nixon impeachment claim
item.description:
A close political ally of Donald Trump, Roger Stone, has publicly called for the president to remove all staff members of the Smithsonian Institution following a controversy involving how the museum system described Richard Nixon’s impeachment history. The dispute centers on an allegation that a Smithsonian exhibit or related material incorrectly stated that Nixon was impeached during the Watergate era.
Stone, a longtime Trump confidant and political operative known for his loyalty to Nixon, posted on social media that Trump should “fire everyone at the Smithsonian” over what he characterized as a false historical claim. He further demanded that the individuals responsible for the alleged error be publicly identified. His comments were accompanied by imagery and references to the Smithsonian’s National Postal Museum and a headline suggesting the institution had misrepresented Nixon’s impeachment history.
Historically, Richard Nixon was never impeached. Although impeachment proceeding

item.title: Overview of the Los Angeles City Attorney and Controller Races for 2026
item.description: As Los Angeles voters head to the polls, two key city positions are drawing attention: the City Attorney and the City Controller. Incumbent City Attorney Hydee Feldstein Soto is running for a second term, highlighting her efforts to take a tough stance on crime and improve oversight at City Hall. Her challengers, including Aida Ashouri, John McKinney, and Marissa Roy, argue that she has mismanaged the office, citing rising legal payouts and controversies over deleted text messages and telework policies. Support from influential groups, like the police union backing McKinney, has added intensity to the race. Meanwhile, the City Controller race features incumbent Kenneth Mejia, who touts his first-term achievements in improving accountability and fiscal scrutiny at City Hall. His challenger, Zach Sokoloff, claims that stronger oversight is still needed to ensure government efficiency and proper use of public funds. These elections, alongside other city contests, have attracted attention

item.title: Brooke Shields’ Childhood: Early Fame, Family Life, and Career Beginnings
item.description: Brooke Shields, born in Manhattan in 1965, had a childhood heavily shaped by early fame and family dynamics. Her parents, actress Teri Shields and businessman Francis Alexander 'Frank' Shields, divorced when Brooke was just five months old, leaving her mother as her primary caregiver and manager. Brooke’s upbringing combined contrasting worlds: her mother’s working-class New Jersey background, marked by devotion but complicated by alcoholism, and her father’s more affluent, aristocratic lineage. From an early age, Brooke was immersed in modeling, starting at 11 months old with an Ivory Soap ad photographed by Francesco Scavullo. By age three, she was doing runway work, eventually appearing on a Vogue cover at 14. Acting followed closely behind, with early roles in films like 'Alice, Sweet Alice' and her breakthrough in 'Pretty Baby' at age 12. The controversial role in 'Pretty Baby' and later films like 'The Blue Lagoon' and 'Endless Love' exposed her to public scrutiny and sexualizati

item.title: Guide to Accessing the May 31 Crossword and Its Solutions by Evan Birnholz
item.description: Evan Birnholz, a contributor to the Washington Post, has provided the solution to his May 31 crossword titled 'After-School Activities.' The crossword celebrates the post-graduate experience and is part of the newspaper's regular feature. For online solvers, there is now an option to print a blank PDF of the crossword as it appears in the newspaper edition. By clicking on the Print icon above the grid, users can access 'Evan’s Sunday print options,' where they can choose 'Empty' to get a blank grid for solving or 'Solution' to see the completed answers. This option is distinct from other print options listed below and is intended to make it easier for enthusiasts to engage with the puzzle offline. The announcement emphasizes the convenience for solvers who prefer a physical version and ensures that users select the correct buttons to obtain either the blank puzzle or the solution. This update highlights the Washington Post’s efforts to enhance accessibility for readers who enjoy crossw

item.title: Trump Seeks Control Over Presidential Records to Block Future Investigations
item.description: Former and current U.S. President Donald Trump is actively working to claim personal ownership over his presidential records, a move legal experts warn could create a lasting legal barrier against investigations. A Justice Department opinion released in April, authored by a Trump loyalist, argued that the Presidential Records Act of 1978 is unconstitutional because it infringes on the executive branch's autonomy. This opinion would give Trump sole authority to decide which records are preserved or made public. Legal scholars, including former National Archives litigation director Jason R. Baron, have expressed concern that if Trump succeeds, he could block future subpoenas, preventing public access to records. Federal courts have already pushed back: Judge John D. Bates of the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia rejected Trump’s arguments in May, quoting George Orwell to underline the stakes of controlling historical records. This ongoing battle has roots in Trump’s first

item.title: Critics challenge Trump’s interpretation of cognitive screening test results
item.description:
The article reports on a wave of criticism following a late-night social media post by Donald Trump in which he claimed to have achieved a perfect score on a cognitive screening test and presented it as evidence of “extreme intelligence.” Trump stated that he had taken the test four times and accumulated a perfect score each time, using the results to suggest superior cognitive ability and to criticize political opponents. However, medical professionals and commentators quickly disputed his interpretation of the test and its implications.
Experts clarified that the Montreal Cognitive Assessment (MoCA), which Trump referenced, is not an intelligence or IQ test. Instead, it is a brief screening tool used by healthcare providers to detect potential signs of cognitive impairment or dementia. A score in the upper range (26–30) is generally considered normal cognitive function, not an indicator of exceptional intelligence. Critics emphasized that repeatedly taking such a screening test in a

item.title: Debate Emerges Over Inflation Metrics as Trump Fed Nominee Kevin Warsh Faces Criticism
item.description:
A former insider has accused Donald Trump’s Federal Reserve chair nominee, Kevin Warsh, of using misleading methods to present inflation data in a more favorable light. The controversy stems from Warsh’s reported support for adopting a “trimmed mean” inflation measure instead of the widely used core Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index. According to reporting cited in the article, the trimmed mean approach excludes extreme price movements, resulting in a lower inflation reading—about 2.3 percent compared to the 3.3 percent shown by core PCE over the past year.
Critics, including former Georgia congresswoman Marjorie Taylor Greene, argue that this shift in measurement would obscure the real cost pressures faced by consumers, particularly in essential categories like food and energy. Greene characterized the proposed change as similar to “common core” math, implying it manipulates data presentation to reach a politically convenient outcome. She suggested that such adjustments c

item.title: Analysis: Trump-linked America 250 celebration faces setbacks amid cancellations and controversy
item.description:
A commentary piece examines the growing difficulties surrounding a multi-day U.S. 250th anniversary celebration project linked to President Donald Trump. The event, known as the Great American State Fair and organized in part through the Trump-affiliated Freedom 250 organization, was initially envisioned as a major patriotic festival featuring live music and large-scale festivities. However, it has reportedly encountered significant setbacks, including the withdrawal of nearly all scheduled performers after learning of the event’s political associations.
Former presidential speechwriter David Frum, writing in The Atlantic, suggested that the event’s unraveling reflects deeper issues tied to Trump’s leadership style. He argued that what should have been a straightforward public relations success for a former president known for showmanship instead became a symbol of mismanagement. According to the analysis, the celebration was intended to allow Trump to present himself as a unifying na

item.title: U.S. Evaluates Use of Cold War Plutonium Stockpiles as Fuel for Advanced Nuclear Reactors
item.description:
The United States is reportedly exploring the possibility of repurposing plutonium recovered from dismantled Cold War-era nuclear warheads as fuel for next-generation nuclear reactors. The initiative is being considered amid concerns over long-term uranium supply constraints and growing reliance on foreign sources of enriched uranium. According to the report, the Department of Energy has shortlisted five nuclear companies, including firms developing small modular reactors (SMRs), to assess the feasibility of using this material for civilian nuclear energy production.
The plutonium in question comes from an estimated surplus of around 50 tons, originally intended to be diluted and permanently disposed of through burial. However, the disposal strategy was paused after policy changes, opening the door to potential commercial use of a portion of the stockpile. Reports suggest that up to 20 tons could eventually be made available to private industry.
The proposal has drawn significant cri

item.title: June 7 Webinar Highlights Global Protests Against Military Bases and Militarization
item.description:
This article announces a scheduled online webinar focused on international activism against military bases and broader militarization. The event is presented as a two-hour session including presentations and a Q&A segment, featuring speakers from multiple countries such as South Korea, the Philippines, New Zealand, Australia, Italy, Japan, the United Kingdom, Germany, Spain, France, Ireland, and the Netherlands. The webinar is organized under the umbrella of Global Women for Peace United Against NATO and supported by various anti-war and disarmament organizations.
The central theme of the event is opposition to the expansion and presence of military bases worldwide, which organizers frame as part of a wider system of imperialism, capitalism, land exploitation, and environmental harm. The article argues that militarization contributes to climate change, pollution, and the transformation of civilian communities into strategic targets. It emphasizes grassroots resistance movements led by

item.title: Weekly Highlights from Moss & Fog: Wildlife, Nature Blooms, and Art Installations (May 25–31, 2026)
item.description:
This weekly edition of Moss & Fog’s “Fog Signals” curates a selection of visually striking and unusual stories spanning nature, wildlife, and contemporary art. The issue highlights the aardwolf, a nocturnal African mammal related to hyenas, known for its unusual diet of termites—consuming an astonishing 200,000 to 300,000 termites each night. In the realm of insects, macro photography reveals the intricate beauty of moths, exposing fine details that are typically invisible to the naked eye and showcasing their unexpected visual complexity.
The natural world is also represented through the seasonal transformation of Namaqualand in South Africa, where arid landscapes burst into vibrant color following rainfall, turning a normally barren desert into a spectacular floral display. In an underwater environmental art project near Miami, artist Leandro Erlich has placed 22 concrete cars on the seafloor as part of ReefLine, an artificial reef and sculpture park designed to encourage coral gro

item.title: Rob Schneider uses 'Waterboy' character on Fox News to support Los Angeles mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt
item.description:
Comedian Rob Schneider made a politically charged appearance on Fox News on May 31, 2026, where he endorsed Republican mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt while invoking a well-known character from his film career. During the segment, host Peter Doocy prompted Schneider to respond in the voice of his character from the 1998 comedy “The Waterboy,” known for the catchphrase “you can do it.” Schneider complied, delivering the line while also expressing distrust of Democrats, which drew an enthusiastic reaction from Doocy.
The endorsement comes as Pratt, a Republican candidate, attempts to unseat incumbent Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass. Despite receiving backing from several right-leaning public figures, Pratt remains behind Bass in recent polling. Schneider’s performance blended entertainment with political messaging, highlighting the increasing overlap between celebrity culture and political advocacy.
The moment was shared widely on social media, where reactions varied depending on politica

item.title: Virginia Governor Abigail Spanberger Faces Backlash Over Vetoes
item.description: Virginia Governor Abigail Spanberger has sparked controversy within her own Democratic Party by vetoing 31 bills passed by the Democratic-led legislature, actions she considers moderate. The vetoes include a bill that would have allowed public employees to engage in collective bargaining, prompting protests in Richmond. Angela Arrington, a 60-year-old first-time voter and former supporter of Spanberger, expressed her disappointment during a recent demonstration, highlighting the growing frustration among constituents who expected the governor to support labor rights and progressive measures. Spanberger’s decisions have created tension among lawmakers and activists, as many Democrats feel betrayed by her conservative approach to governance despite her party affiliation. The protests reflect a broader concern that her moderate stance may not align with the priorities of her voter base, raising questions about her future political support. While Spanberger defends her actions as responsib

item.title: Former Trump campaign adviser warns of potential wave of congressional subpoenas if House control shifts
item.description:
David Urban, a Republican strategist who previously served as senior campaign adviser to Donald Trump during the 2016 election, issued a stark warning about the political consequences the president could face ahead of the 2026 midterm elections. Speaking on CNN, Urban responded to Trump’s recent comments in which the president said he did not “care about the midterms,” particularly in the context of ongoing foreign policy tensions involving Iran and domestic political challenges.
Urban argued that voters are increasingly sensitive to economic pressures, such as rising costs for fuel and everyday expenses, which could shape the outcome of the midterm elections. He suggested that if Republicans lose control of the House of Representatives, Trump and his administration could soon face an aggressive legislative oversight campaign led by Democrats. According to Urban, this would likely include a surge of congressional subpoenas targeting individuals and organizations connected to Trump’s p

item.title: Zig project restricts AI-generated code contributions due to quality and review burden concerns
item.description: The open-source programming language project Zig has announced a policy banning AI-assisted code contributions, citing concerns about quality and the strain such submissions place on maintainers. Zig is designed as a modern alternative to C, with an emphasis on performance and improved memory safety, and is maintained by a nonprofit foundation supported by a distributed community of contributors. According to Zig president Andrew Kelley, who discussed the issue on the JetBrains podcast, the project has been receiving a growing number of AI-generated pull requests that often lack practical value. He described many of these contributions as not only unhelpful but actively harmful, since they require time and effort from reviewers to evaluate and reject them. At the time of the discussion, Zig reportedly had around 200 open pull requests, and maintainers argued that the influx of low-quality submissions slows down development and diverts attention from meaningful work. Kelley emphasized t

item.title: Mike Pence criticizes Trump-backed DOJ compensation fund for alleged wrongful prosecutions
item.description:
Former Vice President Mike Pence publicly broke with the Trump administration over a controversial $1.8 billion Department of Justice–related fund aimed at compensating individuals who claim they were unfairly investigated or prosecuted by the government. In comments made during an interview with NBC News’ “Meet the Press,” Pence described the initiative as both “deeply offensive” and a “bad idea,” urging the administration to abandon it entirely.
The fund has drawn significant legal and political scrutiny. It is designed to provide compensation to people who argue they were wrongfully targeted by government investigations, including individuals involved in the January 6, 2021 attack on the U.S. Capitol. Pence, who was present at the Capitol during the attack, has been a consistent critic of efforts to rehabilitate or financially support participants in the riot.
Legal challenges have intensified in recent days. A federal judge in Florida reopened an underlying IRS-related case after

item.title: GOP strategists warn Trump faces mounting political and economic challenges ahead of midterms
item.description:
Republican strategists are raising alarms about President Donald Trump’s political standing as the 2026 midterm elections approach, citing both economic concerns and internal party tensions. During a CNN appearance, GOP strategist Brad Todd warned that Trump has a narrow window—approximately 30 days—to stabilize his position or risk significant electoral losses for Republicans. Todd pointed to declining favorability ratings and public backlash tied to Trump’s policies, particularly tariffs and a military conflict with Iran, which have reportedly contributed to rising consumer prices and economic strain for many Americans.
In addition to external pressures, Todd emphasized growing discord within the Republican Party itself. He noted that eight out of 53 Republican senators are currently dissatisfied with the White House, a situation he described as problematic given the need to pass legislation. According to Todd, many of these issues are self-inflicted and could have been avoided with

item.title: Critics from across the political spectrum challenge Maria Bartiromo’s claims about Kennedy Center naming dispute
item.description:
Fox News host Maria Bartiromo faced criticism from journalists and commentators across the political spectrum after making inaccurate claims about a legal dispute involving the naming of the Kennedy Center. In a social media post, Bartiromo defended former President Donald Trump’s reported effort to attach his name to the institution, asserting that the Kennedy Center board had already approved the change. She also accused a federal judge of acting out of political bias, describing the ruling against the change as part of a broader “Get Trump” effort.
However, multiple observers quickly pointed out that her central claim was incorrect. Legal experts and journalists emphasized that the authority to rename the Kennedy Center does not lie with its board but with Congress, as established by federal law. The judge’s ruling, they noted, simply upheld that legal framework. Among those criticizing Bartiromo were former Washington Post media reporter Paul Farhi and conservative commentator Pra

item.title: A Glimpse into Clint Eastwood’s Early Life and Career During His Rawhide Years
item.description:
The article revisits a series of candid photographs taken in 1961 that offer a rare, intimate look at Clint Eastwood during the early stages of his career. At the time, Eastwood was 31 years old and beginning to gain recognition through his role as Rowdy Yates on the popular CBS television western Rawhide, which aired from 1959 to 1965. The show provided him with steady work, financial stability, and increasing visibility, marking his transition from a struggling actor to a rising television personality.
Beyond his on-screen success, the images highlight Eastwood’s personal life in Los Angeles, where he lived with his first wife, Maggie Johnson. These photographs portray a more relaxed and domestic side of the actor, contrasting with his tough cowboy persona on television. They capture a moment in time when Eastwood had not yet become the global film icon he would later be known as.
The article also touches on a lesser-known aspect of his early career: his brief attempt at music. In

item.title: Democratic leaders propose taxing Trump compensation fund payouts to block recipients from benefiting
item.description:
Democratic governors and lawmakers across several U.S. states are advancing a coordinated strategy to counter a $1.8 billion fund associated with former President Donald Trump. The fund, described by critics as an “anti-weaponization fund,” was reportedly established as part of a settlement tied to Trump dropping a $10 billion lawsuit against the Internal Revenue Service over leaked tax records. It is intended to compensate individuals who claim they were unfairly targeted by the Justice Department during the Biden administration, including some participants convicted for their roles in the January 6 Capitol attack.
In response, Democratic officials are proposing to impose state-level taxes of up to 100% on any payouts received from the fund. New York Assemblymember Alex Bores has introduced legislation aimed at ensuring that residents who receive money from the fund would effectively forfeit it through taxation. Governors Gavin Newsom of California and Kathy Hochul of New York have e

item.title: WHO Reports First Recoveries in Congo’s Ebola Outbreak Amid Ongoing Health Crisis
item.description: The Democratic Republic of the Congo is experiencing a new outbreak of the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola, a rare variant for which there is no approved vaccine. WHO Director-General Tedros Ghebreyesus confirmed that five patients have recovered, marking the first documented recoveries in the current outbreak. The outbreak has already caused 134 confirmed cases and 18 deaths across the Congo and Uganda, with hundreds more suspected. Ghebreyesus visited Bunia, the epicenter, emphasizing the need for community cooperation and calling for local militias to declare a ceasefire to help health efforts. The region is challenged by decades-long ethnic conflict between the Hema and Lendu groups, which complicates containment and treatment efforts. Meanwhile, the U.S. is sending exposed Americans to a quarantine and treatment center in Kenya rather than bringing them home, sparking debate over access to specialized care. The WHO is working with partners to scale up diagnostic capacity and support tr

item.title: Thom Hartmann criticizes corporate personhood and its influence on U.S. elections
item.description:
The article is an opinion piece by Thom Hartmann arguing that the modern U.S. electoral system has been significantly distorted by the legal doctrine of corporate personhood. Hartmann claims that historical legal interpretations of the Fourteenth Amendment, particularly rooted in the late 19th century, have been misused to extend constitutional protections originally intended for individuals to corporations. He traces this development to the 1886 Santa Clara County v. Southern Pacific Railroad case, arguing that a clerk’s headnote, rather than the Court’s formal ruling, helped establish the idea that corporations could be treated as “persons” under the Constitution. According to the article, this interpretation evolved over time through subsequent Supreme Court decisions such as First National Bank v. Bellotti and later Citizens United v. FEC, which expanded corporate political spending rights under the First Amendment.
Hartmann argues that these rulings have enabled wealthy corporati

item.title: Intermittent fasting linked to coordinated changes in gut bacteria and brain activity during weight loss
item.description:
A study published in Frontiers examined how an intermittent energy restriction (IER) diet affects both the body and brain in adults with obesity. Researchers followed 25 participants in China with an average age of 27 and BMI ranging from 28 to 45. The intervention included a 32-day highly controlled fasting phase, during which calorie intake was gradually reduced, followed by a 30-day low-calorie phase where participants consumed around 500–600 calories per day depending on sex.
By the end of the program, participants lost an average of 7.6 kilograms, or about 7.8% of their body weight. They also showed improvements in metabolic health markers, including reduced blood pressure, fasting glucose, cholesterol levels, and liver enzyme activity. Beyond physical changes, brain imaging using functional MRI revealed decreased activity in regions associated with appetite regulation, reward processing, addiction-related behavior, and self-control.
At the same time, researchers observed signif

item.title: Fernando Tatis Jr hits first home run of the season after 55-game drought
item.description: San Diego Padres star Fernando Tatis Jr finally broke his 55-game home run drought, the second-longest for a player who hits 40 or more home runs in a season. On May 30, 2026, Tatis hit a solo home run off Washington Nationals pitcher Foster Griffin, traveling 451 feet. This homer came after a streak of 207 at-bats without a home run, a period that began after his last homer on September 27 against the Arizona Diamondbacks. Despite the milestone, the Padres lost to the Nationals 9-4. Tatis, a three-time All-Star, is currently batting .271 with a .670 OPS, including six doubles, a triple, and now one home run. His teammate Xander Bogaerts noted that the home run would relieve some of the pressure Tatis felt, allowing him to sleep better. The long-awaited home run received enthusiastic cheers from the crowd and provided a notable highlight in an otherwise challenging season start for the star player.

item.title: Demographic shifts and voter trends make Texas Senate race more competitive for Republicans
item.description:
Republicans are increasingly concerned about the political future of Texas as demographic and electoral changes make the traditionally conservative state appear more competitive in upcoming Senate elections. According to reporting cited by Axios and referenced in the article, a combination of population growth, shifting voter behavior, and changing demographics has “scrambled the political math” in a state that has long been a Republican stronghold. Texas has seen more than 2.5 million new residents since 2020, many of whom are described by political scientists as either economic migrants or political “refugees” from other states. Experts argue that these newcomers are less tied to Texas’ traditional political alignment, potentially giving Democrats new opportunities in statewide races.
Another key factor is the evolving voting behavior of Hispanic Texans, now the largest demographic group in the state. While Hispanic voters helped contribute to Republican gains in previous elections,

item.title: Physician questions frequency and interpretation of Trump’s cognitive screening results
item.description:
A physician appearing on MS NOW on Sunday raised concerns about former President Donald Trump’s recent claims regarding his cognitive testing, suggesting that the circumstances surrounding the exams may point to medical caution rather than routine evaluation. The discussion followed Trump’s early-morning post on Truth Social, in which he stated he had achieved a perfect score on the Montreal Cognitive Assessment (MoCA), describing it as a highly difficult cognitive test and using the result to argue he demonstrates strong intelligence.
Dr. Vin Gupta pushed back on this framing, explaining that the MoCA is primarily a screening tool designed to detect early signs of cognitive decline or dementia, rather than a measure of executive functioning or the ability to perform complex leadership tasks. He added that the test is generally not considered difficult in the way Trump described.
Gupta further emphasized that such cognitive screenings are typically initiated by physicians rather th

item.title: Nancy Mace criticized by allies and opponents after public appeal for Trump’s support
item.description:
Republican Representative Nancy Mace drew criticism from both supporters of former President Donald Trump and her political opponents after publicly emphasizing her loyalty to him in a recent social media post. Mace, who is currently running for governor of South Carolina, highlighted what she described as a “100% lifetime voting record supporting Trump” following reports that Trump had already endorsed her rival in the race.
The post appeared to be an attempt to regain favor with Trump and his base, but it instead triggered backlash from several prominent voices within the MAGA movement. Influential pro-Trump commentator Catturd openly mocked the message, noting that Trump had not endorsed Mace. Former Congressman George Santos also criticized the post as misleading, arguing that Mace has lost credibility among many Trump supporters due to past controversies and erratic behavior.
Criticism extended beyond Trump allies. Conservative commentator Jonah Goldberg ridiculed the tone of Ma

item.title: Goofy-footed surfers lead rankings as early CT season reshapes competitive standings
item.description:
This article analyzes the early stages of the Championship Tour (CT) season in professional surfing, focusing on emerging trends in performance and rankings after the first third of competition. A key storyline highlighted is the strong presence of goofy-footed surfers at the top of the leaderboard, suggesting that current conditions and event locations may be favoring athletes who naturally ride with their right foot forward. This unexpected pattern has sparked discussion about whether wave selection, event design, or evolving competitive strategies are influencing outcomes more than individual star power alone.
At the same time, the piece notes a striking contrast: the surfer widely regarded as one of the best in the world is currently positioned in 10th place. This gap between reputation and ranking underscores the volatility of the current season, where consistency appears to be just as important as peak performance. The article frames this as part of a broader competitive shift i

item.title: Cardiologist raises concerns and questions about details in Trump’s latest medical exam
item.description:
A prominent cardiologist, Dr. Jonathan Reiner, publicly raised several questions regarding President Donald Trump’s most recent medical examination, despite the White House physician’s conclusion that the president is in good overall health. Reiner focused particularly on the decision to conduct a second coronary artery CT scan only six months after a prior scan, noting that such follow-up imaging is uncommon unless there are concerns stemming from earlier findings. He asked what prompted the repeat test.
Reiner outlined a series of additional issues he believes warrant clarification. He pointed to an apparent inconsistency in the report’s description of Trump’s ankle swelling, which was said to have improved compared to the previous year, even though no swelling had been documented in the earlier exam. He also questioned why Trump is reportedly taking a dosage of aspirin significantly higher than standard recommendations for cardiovascular prevention.
Other concerns included unexpla

item.title: Report warns of algae risk in Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool amid unresolved infrastructure issues
item.description:
Renovations to the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington, D.C., overseen by the Trump administration, have drawn scrutiny after reports highlighted a persistent infrastructure problem that has not been resolved. According to documents cited by The New York Times, the system responsible for circulating and filtering the pool’s roughly four million gallons of water depends on a network of underground 12-inch pipes that move water to a treatment facility and back. When functioning properly, this system filters the entire pool every three days. However, the pipes have repeatedly broken or leaked, with officials noting that their plastic construction can fail under pressure from surrounding soil. When this occurs, the filtration system must be shut down, leaving the pool stagnant for extended periods, sometimes weeks at a time.
Experts warn that if the underlying issue is not addressed, algae growth could return, potentially causing the pool’s blue surface to be obscured by a thic

item.title: Trump’s Foreign Policy Struggles Highlight Challenges in the Middle East
item.description: The article discusses former President Donald Trump's aggressive and confrontational approach to international relations, comparing it to schoolyard bullying. It highlights his domestic and foreign actions, from demonizing undocumented workers to engaging in military strikes abroad. The piece focuses on a recent conflict with Iran, where Trump's expectations of a quick collapse were met with strong Iranian resistance, including missile attacks on U.S. bases, regional infrastructure, and Israel. After an extended period of failed military intervention, Trump turned his attention to Oman, a smaller nation controlling part of the Strait of Hormuz. The article argues that Trump's threats toward Oman demonstrate his diminished influence following the unsuccessful campaign against Iran. It concludes that his reputation as a powerful global figure has been weakened, with long-term consequences for U.S. credibility and international relations. The author also emphasizes the broader impact of T

item.title: Clinical trial shows pancreatic cancer drug significantly extends survival compared to chemotherapy
item.description:
A new clinical trial has revealed encouraging results for a long-anticipated pancreatic cancer treatment called daraxonrasib, a drug that has been in development for more than four decades. According to the findings, patients treated with the pill experienced significantly improved outcomes compared with those receiving standard chemotherapy, with survival and tumor control lasting roughly twice as long.
Pancreatic cancer is widely regarded as one of the most aggressive and difficult-to-treat forms of cancer, often diagnosed at advanced stages and associated with poor survival rates. Because of this, progress in treatment has been slow and incremental, making this new development particularly notable for researchers and clinicians. The trial results suggest that daraxonrasib may represent a meaningful step forward in managing the disease, offering patients additional time and improved disease stability.
Researchers involved in the study emphasized that while the results are promisin

item.title: Trump’s Primary Battles Could Complicate Approval of Future Health Nominees
item.description:
An analysis highlighted in The Hill suggests that former President Donald Trump’s efforts to reshape the Republican Party by backing primary challengers against perceived disloyal lawmakers may create unintended consequences for his administration. One key example is Senator Bill Cassidy of Louisiana, who lost his GOP primary to a Trump-supported candidate after previously voting to convict Trump following the January 6 Capitol attack and urging him to withdraw from the 2024 race. Despite his electoral defeat, Cassidy remains chair of the Senate Health Committee, a powerful position that oversees confirmation of major federal health appointments.
This role gives Cassidy significant influence over upcoming nominations, including candidates for director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, U.S. surgeon general, and leadership at the Food and Drug Administration. Observers note that Cassidy could use this authority to scrutinize or potentially block nominees aligned with Tr

item.title: Appeals Court Pressure Mounts Over Delayed Release of Trump Classified Documents Report
item.description:
Former U.S. Attorney Joyce Vance has suggested that legal pressure is intensifying around former President Donald Trump in connection with the classified documents investigation. The focus of the dispute is Volume II of Special Counsel Jack Smith’s report, which addresses classified materials found at Mar-a-Lago. U.S. District Judge Aileen Cannon, who was appointed by Trump, has blocked the release of this portion of the report since Inauguration Day and delayed action on requests from advocacy groups such as American Oversight and the Knight First Amendment Institute, which seek its publication.
The Eleventh Circuit Court of Appeals previously criticized Cannon for what it described as “undue delay” and ordered her to issue a ruling within 60 days. After she ruled against releasing the report, the groups appealed again. The appellate court has now set a full briefing schedule, with submissions due by July, signaling a more active role in resolving the matter.
Vance interprets these

item.title: How Turkey Became a Global Leader in Hair Transplant Innovation and Medical Tourism
item.description:
The article explores how Turkey became the world’s leading hub for hair transplants through a combination of medical innovation, engineering adaptation, and evolving industry dynamics. What began in the late 1990s as an effort to reverse medical tourism flows—bringing patients to Turkey instead of sending Turkish celebrities abroad—has grown into a multibillion-dollar global industry. Turkey now attracts millions of medical tourists annually, with a significant share seeking aesthetic procedures such as hair restoration.
The growth of the sector is explained through three phases of “health tourism evolution.” In its early stage, institutional hospitals built strong clinical standards and training systems, establishing Turkey as a trusted destination for high-quality procedures. The second phase expanded through a master–apprentice model, where surgeons trained in high-volume clinics later opened boutique practices, spreading expertise and increasing surgical capacity.
By the mid-2010

item.title: California Governor Race: Scrutiny of Xavier Becerra’s Career and Competence
item.description: Xavier Becerra, the front-runner in California's gubernatorial primary, is facing criticism over his record in the Biden administration, particularly as Health and Human Services Secretary. Opponents, including Democratic rival Tom Steyer, have questioned Becerra’s handling of the COVID-19 pandemic and unaccompanied migrant children, citing allegations of mismanagement and incompetence. Much of this criticism comes from anonymous former administration officials, making motivations unclear. Public figures like Ron Klain and Neera Tanden defend Becerra, highlighting achievements such as negotiating lower prescription drug costs, expanding healthcare coverage under the Affordable Care Act, and advocating for minors in the Office of Refugee Resettlement. The article notes that Becerra entered the Biden administration after the pandemic response was underway and that his role in immigration was limited compared to Homeland Security. While some investigative reports raise legitimate concerns

item.title: Finance Ministry Highlights Economic Risks from Geopolitical Tensions and Energy Prices
item.description: India's Finance Ministry emphasized the need for policy flexibility to sustain economic growth and manage inflation amid ongoing geopolitical tensions in West Asia. In its May economic review, the ministry highlighted that disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz pose the most significant risk to India's external and price stability, as roughly a fifth of global energy passes through this strategic waterway. Rising global energy prices, a depreciating rupee, and upstream cost pressures are already influencing inflation, while a potential poor monsoon could further exacerbate food price increases, affecting rural demand and overall growth. The ministry clarified that the falling rupee should not be seen as a reflection of weak macroeconomic fundamentals but as an adjustment from a position of relative strength. Robust services exports, strong foreign exchange reserves, record foreign direct investment inflows, and a stable labor market are cited as pillars of resilience. Policy measures, inc

item.title: Meteor Likely Caused Sonic Boom Felt Across New England
item.description:
A loud sonic boom that startled residents across parts of New England on Saturday afternoon was likely caused by a meteor entering Earth’s atmosphere, according to meteorologists. Police departments in Massachusetts received numerous reports of a powerful explosion-like sound, prompting concern among residents who also reported feeling their homes shake.
Meteorologist Pamela Gardner indicated that satellite data from the GOES-19 weather satellite suggested the presence of a meteor or bolide—an object that explodes as it travels through the atmosphere. She noted that there was no lightning activity or seismic event recorded at the time, ruling out thunderstorms and earthquakes as possible causes.
WBZ-TV Chief Meteorologist Eric Fisher supported this explanation, explaining that meteors can appear in lightning detection systems because their atmospheric entry produces similar energy signatures. He pointed to lightning-mapping satellite data that helped identify the likely path of the o

item.title: Profile of Italian actress Rossana Podestà and her legacy in 1950s–60s European cinema
item.description:
Rossana Podestà (1934–2013) was an Italian actress who rose to prominence during the golden age of European cinema in the 1950s and 1960s. Known for her luminous green eyes, natural elegance, and warm screen presence, she became one of the most admired film stars of her generation. Her beauty and charisma helped define the Mediterranean ideal of cinematic femininity during this era, earning her international recognition beyond Italy.
Podestà achieved global fame in 1956 when she portrayed Helen of Troy in the Hollywood epic “Helen of Troy,” a role that significantly boosted her international profile. This performance established her as a recognizable figure in both European and American film industries. Following this breakthrough, she continued to appear in a variety of successful productions throughout the late 1950s and 1960s.
Among her notable films are “The Seventh Voyage of Sinbad” (1958), a popular fantasy adventure film, and “Sodom and Gomorrah” (1962), an epic historical pro

item.title: San Antonio Spurs Defeat Thunder in Game 7 to Advance to NBA Finals
item.description:
The San Antonio Spurs secured a 111-103 victory over the Oklahoma City Thunder in Game 7 of the Western Conference Finals, earning their first trip to the NBA Finals since 2014. Led by Victor Wembanyama’s 22-point performance, the Spurs delivered a balanced offensive effort, with Julian Champagnie contributing 20 points—18 from three-point range—and multiple players scoring in double figures. Stephon Castle added 16 points, while De’Aaron Fox, Dylan Harper, Keldon Johnson, and Devin Vassell also played key supporting roles.
The decisive game was tightly contested, unlike earlier games in the series that were largely settled before the fourth quarter. San Antonio held a narrow 80-77 lead entering the final period after both teams exchanged momentum swings throughout the game. The Spurs had earlier built leads of up to 14 points in the first half and 11 in the third quarter, only for the Thunder to repeatedly fight back.
A pivotal moment came midway through the fourth quarter when Luke

item.title: Lawsuit Filed After Child Contracts Severe E. coli Infection Linked to Kebab Shop Beef
item.description:
A California family has filed a lawsuit against a kebab restaurant chain and its beef supplier after a child became seriously ill with an E. coli infection allegedly linked to beef kofta consumed at one of the chain’s locations in Los Angeles. According to the complaint, the child ate minced beef kofta at The Kebab Shop in early April and developed severe gastrointestinal symptoms, including nausea, vomiting, fatigue, and bloody diarrhea within days. He was later hospitalized at UCLA medical facilities, where tests confirmed a Shiga toxin-producing E. coli infection. His condition worsened and progressed to hemolytic uremic syndrome (HUS), a life-threatening complication that can cause kidney failure. The child required intensive care, including dialysis, blood transfusions, and treatment for seizures and organ complications.
The lawsuit, filed in Los Angeles County Superior Court, targets both TKS Restaurants, the parent company of The Kebab Shop, and Olympia Food Industries, the Ill

item.title: Transgender athlete AB Hernandez wins two California state track titles in final high school meet
item.description: Transgender athlete AB Hernandez of Jurupa Valley High School concluded a highly-publicized high school track and field career at the 2026 California State Championships in Clovis. Hernandez secured first place in both the girls' high jump and triple jump events, and finished third in the long jump. This mirrored last year’s achievements, where Hernandez won two state championships and placed second in the long jump. For the second consecutive year, the California Interscholastic Federation (CIF) allowed podium recognition for female competitors finishing directly behind Hernandez. Images of Hernandez sharing podiums with female athletes sparked widespread debate over transgender participation in girls' sports, attracting both supporters and critics. During the championship weekend, political figures weighed in: Democrat Tom Steyer appeared in a video with Hernandez, while Republican Steve Hilton held a rally advocating for girls' sports protection. A press conference organized by loca

item.title: Nationals Overcome Padres with Late-Game Surge in 9-4 Victory
item.description: The Washington Nationals secured a 9-4 victory over the San Diego Padres on Saturday, thanks to a decisive sixth-run seventh inning. For the first six innings, Nationals batters carefully faced Padres ace Michael King, avoiding risky swings and waiting for the right opportunities. In the seventh inning, Washington capitalized on a combination of singles, walks, hit batters, and fielder’s choices, producing six runs and turning the game in their favor. The Nationals showcased determination and athleticism by running aggressively, sliding headfirst, and keeping the offense moving efficiently. This win marks the potential continuation of their recent strong performance, as they aim to win or split their sixth consecutive series. With solid teamwork and strategic patience, the Nationals demonstrated their ability to overcome a tough pitcher and gain momentum late in the game.

item.title: U.S. Energy Department Considers Using Surplus Plutonium as Fuel for Civilian Nuclear Reactors
item.description: The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) is considering a program to make surplus plutonium available for use in nuclear fuel, marking a significant shift from its traditional role in weapons production. Up to five companies—Oklo, Exodys Energy, Shine Technologies, Standard Nuclear, and Flibe Energy, Inc.—have been selected for advanced negotiations regarding potential allocations of surplus plutonium. This initiative follows a White House executive order halting a prior program that aimed to dilute and dispose of the material. The DOE's plan includes weapons-grade, fuel-grade, reactor-grade, and mixed plutonium, intended to support the growth of domestic nuclear energy, promote innovation in recycling technologies, and attract private investment in advanced reactors. Proponents argue this could help overcome fuel supply constraints and accelerate the deployment of advanced nuclear technologies. However, critics, including former Energy Secretary Ernest Moniz, warn that introducing plutoniu

item.title: Analysis of small modular reactors and the growing gap between nuclear power and renewable energy expansion
item.description:
The article discusses arguments from energy analyst Michael Liebreich, who challenges the idea that nuclear power—particularly small modular reactors (SMRs)—can realistically compete with rapidly expanding wind and solar energy. According to his analysis, even if SMR technology scales successfully, the nuclear sector would need to deploy around 8,000 small reactors to match the energy output achieved by wind and solar in a single recent year. In contrast, more optimistic projections suggest only a handful of SMRs, possibly around five units, might be operational by 2035, highlighting a significant gap between expectations and real-world deployment timelines.
Liebreich argues that nuclear energy is often promoted by political groups and fossil-fuel-aligned interests that prefer it over renewable technologies, rather than for clear climate effectiveness. He emphasizes that wind and solar are expanding rapidly and becoming more efficient and cost-effective, while nuclear projects remain

item.title: YouTube creators achieve major box office success with two leading horror films
item.description:
This article highlights an emerging trend in the film industry where successful YouTube creators are transitioning into mainstream filmmaking with significant commercial impact. Over a recent weekend, the top two films at the box office were both directed by filmmakers who originally built their audiences on YouTube. The horror film “Backrooms,” directed by Kane Parsons, debuted at number one, earning approximately $38 million on Friday and projected to reach between $80 million and $90 million domestically over the weekend. This marks the strongest opening in A24’s history, surpassing its previous record held by “Civil War,” which opened with $25.7 million.
In second place was “Obsession,” a horror-thriller directed by Curry Barker, which also demonstrated unusual box office behavior. It earned around $8 million on Friday and was expected to reach about $28.5 million for the weekend. Unlike most wide-release films, which typically drop 50% to 70% in subsequent weekends, “Obsession” c

item.title: Paris Saint-Germain Wins 2026 Champions League After Defeating Arsenal
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain (PSG) secured a historic victory by defeating Arsenal to claim the 2026 UEFA Champions League title. The match, a high-stakes showdown between two of Europe's elite football clubs, showcased PSG's strategic gameplay and exceptional performance throughout the season. In a post-match discussion, ESPN journalist Julien Laurens highlighted the significance of this win for PSG, marking another milestone in the club's ambitious history. The French team demonstrated remarkable skill and teamwork, led by key players who have consistently delivered outstanding results this season. Ousmane Dembélé, reflecting on PSG’s golden 2025 campaign, praised the club's resilience, dedication, and the support of its fans, emphasizing the collective effort behind this achievement. This victory reinforces PSG's position as a dominant force in European football and contributes to the ongoing narrative of their pursuit of continental success. The win also adds to the club's growing legacy an

item.title: Rockstar North Employees in the UK Form Union Amid Labor Dispute and Return-to-Office Tensions
item.description:
Workers across Rockstar Games’ UK studios have formally announced the creation of a union, marking a significant development in ongoing labor tensions within the company. The newly formed Rockstar Games Workers Union includes employees from multiple offices, including Rockstar North in Scotland as well as locations in Leeds, London, Edinburgh, Dundee, and Lincoln. Rockstar North, one of the company’s largest studios with around 2,000 employees, has been central to recent disputes.
The union is affiliated with the Independent Workers of Great Britain (IWGB) and was publicly introduced through a video statement released by employees. According to the union, organizing efforts intensified following controversial disciplinary actions taken by Rockstar Games in October 2025, when more than 30 employees were dismissed. The company stated that the firings were due to alleged sharing of confidential information in a public forum. However, union representatives argue that the affected employee

item.title: Paris Police Arrest 45 After Celebrations Turn Violent Following PSG Champions League Win
item.description: Following Paris Saint-Germain’s dramatic victory over Arsenal in the Champions League final on May 30, 2026, celebrations across Paris escalated into disorder. Large crowds gathered near iconic locations, including the Arc de Triomphe and the Champs-Élysées, where some fans set off flares, honked car horns, and engaged in vandalism. Authorities reported that a group of individuals attempted to storm a police station, while vehicles were torched and businesses, including a bakery and a restaurant, were damaged. Police intervened to disperse the crowds, and at least one officer was injured in the disturbances. By 10 p.m. local time, Parisian authorities had arrested 45 people in connection with the unrest. These events follow a similar pattern after PSG’s previous Champions League win, which also led to widespread injuries and arrests. Officials continue to monitor the situation, emphasizing public safety during post-match celebrations. The city’s law enforcement is working to prevent fu
